name: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
summary: Convolutional Neural Networks are a class of neural networks that use learnable convolutional filters to automatically extract hierarchical spatial features from input data, particularly images. CNNs exploit translational invariance through weight sharing — the same filter is applied across all spatial positions. A typical CNN architecture stacks convolutional layers (feature extraction), pooling layers (spatial downsampling), and fully connected layers (classification). CNNs dominated computer vision from AlexNet's 2012 ImageNet breakthrough through the mid-2020s, when Vision Transformers began achieving competitive results.
query_hooks: How do neural networks process images?
